claim: GATA3 maintains the type-2 cytokine program in differentiated cells (not just initiates it)
our_result: GATA3 KO collapses IL13/IL5/IL4; GATA3 ranks #1 on its own axis and #4/6923 in the classifier
evidence: Yamashita et al., J Biol Chem 2004 — 'Essential role of GATA3 for the MAINTENANCE of Th2 cytokine production'; GATA3 ablation in differentiated Th2 reduces all Th2 cytokines

claim: GATA3 drives the type-2 program STAT6-independently (maintenance is STAT6-independent)
our_result: STAT6 and GATA3 signatures anti-correlate (r=-0.41); STAT6 KO does NOT collapse cytokines in circulating committed cells
evidence: Ouyang et al., Immunity 2000 — 'Stat6-INDEPENDENT GATA-3 autoactivation directs IL-4-independent Th2 development and commitment'; GATA3 autoactivation stabilizes Th2 without STAT6

claim: GATA3 can impose the Th2 program even on already-committed cells (autonomous maintenance)
our_result: GATA3-arm is autonomous from STAT6 signaling arm in the network map
evidence: Lee et al., J Exp Med 2000 — ectopic GATA3 induces Th2 cytokines in irreversibly committed Th1 cells; autoregulatory maintenance loop

claim: A chromatin-remodeling module (BAF/SMARCA4-BRG1) sits upstream of the GATA3 cytokine program
our_result: Chromatin module (SMARCA4/KMT2C/BATF) scored upstream of GATA3 (KO reduces GATA3 transcript)
evidence: Linzer et al., Immunity 2023 (CRISPR screen of 1131 TFs) — GATA3/AP-1 need a BRG1/CHD4 chromatin-remodeling bridge (ADNP) to open the type-2 locus; BAF/BRG1 established in Th differentiation

claim: BATF is a bona fide Th2/type-2 differentiation TF (not a screen artifact)
our_result: BATF top chromatin-arm hit; enriched in lesional skin (log2FC 2.17)
evidence: BATF/JunB AP-1 axis regulates IL-4/Th2 gene expression (JunB review Immunol Med 2021; BATF Th2 literature n=6)

claim: IL4R is a central, disease-relevant type-2 node
our_result: IL4R ranks #2 on STAT6 axis, tops integrated priority, strongest GWAS (asthma p=2e-32)
evidence: IL-4/IL-13 receptor biology drives allergic inflammation; dupilumab (anti-IL4Ra) approved in AD; STAT6 dysregulation central to atopic dermatitis (Int Rev Immunol 2024)

claim: A CRISPR TF screen is a valid way to discover Th2 regulators (methodological soundness)
evidence: Linzer et al. 2023 ran a targeted 1,131-TF CRISPR screen for Th2 and recovered GATA3+chromatin biology — same logic, smaller scope; validates the approach
